title: oenothera decorticans var. rutila
intro: Oenothera decorticans var. rutila, also known as a type of evening primrose, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Oenothera decorticans var. rutila include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting overall skin health. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
